Operation 'resolveTicket' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. data.current_description: Mark a support ticket as resolved.
data.description_suggestion: Marks a support ticket as resolved, closing the ticket and updating its status. Requires the ticket ID as input. Returns the updated ticket object with the resolved status and timestamp. This operation changes the ticket state and may trigger notifications to relevant parties.
data.reasoning: Original summary and description are too minimal. The description lacks critical details about required inputs (ticket ID), what is returned (updated ticket object), and important side effects (status change, potential notifications). Enhanced version provides AI agents with clear understanding of when to use this operation, what parameters are needed, and what state changes occur.

message: Operation 'readVendors' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original summary and description lack specificity about capabilities and use cases. The operation has 4 parameters suggesting filtering/pagination support, but this is not mentioned. Enhanced version clarifies that filtering and pagination are available, explains what data is returned, and provides context about when an AI agent should use this operation (vendor lookup, catalog retrieval, integration scenarios).

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of vendor records from the system. Supports filtering and pagination through query parameters. Returns an array of vendor objects containing vendor details such as name, contact information, and service categories. Use this operation to search for specific vendors or retrieve the complete vendor catalog for reference or integration purposes.
